_comment: Out-of-band, on-demand mutation testing (advisory) — `npm run test:mutation` (issue #164). Measures TEST EFFICACY, the axis the 100% coverage gate can't see: it injects faults into the in-scope modules and checks the suite KILLS them (a surviving mutant is a covered-but-weakly-asserted line). NOT a CI gate and NOT part of `npm test` — the nightly/workflow_dispatch advisory job is a separate, currently-blocked follow-up (#166). SCOPE: pure-logic modules whose specs run in a plain node vitest config (vitest.stryker.config.ts — no Cloudflare plugin), because Stryker's vitest-runner re-runs vitest under instrumentation many times and the @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers pool (live miniflare/D1 per file) is not a fit for that loop. That covers nearly all the pure logic — the parsers, the pagination/return-path/log/email helpers, ingest validate + the source registry — since their tests import only `vitest`, the module, and (some) `?raw` fixtures, never `cloudflare:test`/D1. EXCLUDED: (1) the D1 data layer (src/lib/users.ts, src/lib/auth-actions.ts, src/ingest/db.ts + run.ts) and Astro-render-only modules (format.ts, session.ts) — their tests genuinely need the workers pool or a page render; (2) src/lib/auth-crypto.ts — the Web Crypto shell (#228); its tests run fine in node (Node 24 has the same Web Crypto), but each PBKDF2 call is 100k iterations, so mutating it makes the run slow for little marginal value, and the crypto itself can't be meaningfully mutation-tested. Its high-value half — the input validators + the password-record envelope parser — was split out into the pure core src/lib/auth.ts (#228), which IS in scope (tested by test/auth-validate.test.ts + test/auth-validate.prop.test.ts in plain node). Re-scope `mutate` + the include list in vitest.stryker.config.ts together.
reporters_comment: The `json` reporter emits a machine-readable run report to reports/mutation/mutation.json (gitignored), uploaded as the advisory CI job's artifact (.github/workflows/mutation.yml, issue #166) so review tooling can diff the mutation score run-over-run — logs are too brittle to parse. `html`/`clear-text`/`progress` are for the local interactive `npm run test:mutation`.
ignorePatterns_comment: Keep the Stryker sandbox copy lean and skip agent/scratch dirs. `.codex/skills` is a symlink to a directory, which Stryker's sandbox copy (copyfile) can't handle (EISDIR); excluding the dot-dirs and build/test artifacts also speeds up the copy. node_modules is symlinked into the sandbox automatically, so the in-scope source/tests/configs are all Stryker needs.
